hero.subtitle: University of Science and Technology of China InSAR Research Team
hero.description: Advanced InSAR Research for Earth Observation

welcome.title: Welcome to USTC-InSAR Research Team
welcome.paragraph1: We are the USTC-InSAR team from the School of Earth and Space Sciences at the University of Science and Technology of China, led by Professor Xiaohua Xu.
welcome.paragraph2: Our team is dedicated to the development and application of InSAR technology, using satellite radar data to conduct high-precision surface deformation monitoring, earthquake geodesy, and crustal deformation research.
welcome.paragraph3: Our research results are widely published in top international journals, making important contributions to earth science research and natural disaster early warning.

research_areas.title: Research Areas
research_areas.description: We focus on the following key research areas, promoting the development of InSAR technology and earth observation science

insar.title: InSAR Technology and Applications
insar.description: Developing advanced synthetic aperture radar interferometry technology for high-precision surface deformation monitoring.

earthquake.title: Earthquake Geodesy
earthquake.description: Using InSAR data to study earthquake coseismic, post-seismic deformation and earthquake cycle dynamic processes.

crustal.title: Crustal Deformation Research
crustal.description: Studying long-term crustal deformation and tectonic movement in the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau and surrounding areas.

news.title: Latest News
news.description: Stay updated with our team's latest developments

cta.title: Join Us
cta.description: We warmly welcome outstanding students interested in InSAR technology and earth observation research to join our team!

hero.title: Research Results
hero.description: Our research directions and academic publications

areas.title: Research Areas
areas.description: We focus on the following core research directions

insar.title: InSAR Technology and Applications
insar.description: We develop and optimize InSAR processing algorithms to improve the accuracy and efficiency of deformation monitoring. Optimizing InSAR data processing workflows includes automation, phase unwrapping optimization algorithms, time series analysis and other key technologies.

earthquake.title: Earthquake Geodesy
earthquake.description: Using InSAR data to study earthquake coseismic deformation, post-seismic slip, and earthquake cycles. Our research provides important basis for understanding earthquake mechanisms and earthquake hazard assessment.

ai.title: Intersection of InSAR and AI
ai.description: Combining artificial intelligence technology with InSAR data processing, developing intelligent interpretation algorithms, improving data processing efficiency and deformation recognition accuracy, and exploring new InSAR application areas.
